diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Migratory polyarthralgia with fatigue is typical of a lupus articular flare.
Migratory (not fixed monoarticular) pattern argues against septic arthritis.
No pleuritic pain/breathlessness argues strongly against serositis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal urine; no single hot red joint or fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal urine colour/output lowers nephritis probability (urinalysis still ideal).
No single hot red joint or fever argues against septic arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Flare after missed hydroxychloroquine; no new rash/ulcers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Missed hydroxychloroquine is a common flare trigger.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent GP/rheumatology review with bloods (FBC, renal function, complement, dsDNA) and urinalysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess flare activity and screen for subclinical organ involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day assessment if organ/septic-joint red flags develop
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: For serositis, nephritis or a septic joint.
